title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
summary: Some tech executives are nervous that the administration’s recent scrutiny of artificial intelligence models could be a prelude to a demand for an ownership stake.

title: Trump’s Threat to Impose Fees in the Strait of Hormuz Contradicts His Aides
summary: President Trump said the United States could collect tolls or fees, despite Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio saying no country could do so.

title: Will Oil Prices Rise as the US-Iran Truce Frays? The Answer Lies With China.
summary: For decades, OPEC influenced the market by how much oil it produced. But China, the largest importer, is demonstrating its remarkable power over prices.

title: Yemen’s Fragile Peace in Peril as Houthis Accuse Saudi Arabia of Bombing
summary: The Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen accused Saudi Arabia of striking Yemen’s main international airport in a dispute over an Iranian plane that tried to land in the capital, Sana.

title: How Lindsey Graham’s Journey From Trump Critic to Ally Made Him a Power Player
summary: He initially called Donald Trump a “kook” who was “unfit for office,” only to join the new president’s Mar-a-Lago circle. “I’m still in the game,” Mr. Graham once explained of his much-analyzed turnabout.

title: Trump Backs Lindsey Graham’s Sister to Finish His Senate Term in South Carolina
summary: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina will name a replacement for Senator Lindsey Graham, who died on Saturday.

title: Lindsey Graham Had a Complicated Relationship With Black Voters
summary: Mr. Graham, a long-serving senator from South Carolina, was known for his ability to maintain relationships even with his political foes. But the support of Black voters remained elusive.

title: The Next Phase in Ukraine’s War With Russia: The Battle for Minds
summary: Ukrainians, already drone innovators, are turning their focus to psychological operations aimed at undercutting Russian support for the war.

title: A Robot Army Remakes Ground Warfare in Ukraine
summary: They began as supply mules. Now ground robots evacuate the wounded, hold trenches and even do the killing.

title: How a Teacher Revived Backyard Baseball
summary: Backyard Baseball, a favorite game for 1990s kids, had been off the market for years. But Lindsay Barnett was determined.

title: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Is Challenged in Court by California and 11 Other States
summary: The lawsuit, filed by California and 11 other states, argues that the deal would harm movie theaters and damage America’s entertainment industry.

title: Appeals Court Revives Lawsuits Tying Tylenol Use in Pregnancy to Autism and A.D.H.D.
summary: A federal appeals court overruled a district court judge who dismissed the lawsuits, citing unreliable scientific evidence.

title: California Blocked an Oil Pipeline in Santa Barbara. Trump Forced It Open.
summary: The reopening of the pipeline has made residents anxious in a region prized for its gorgeous beaches and ocean views.

title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
summary: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.

title: The eBay Scandal Gets a Thriller-Like Documentary
summary: A newly available documentary, ‘Whatever It Takes,’ examines eBay workers’ plot to cyberstalk journalists and critics.

title: It Takes Coordination (and Many Arms) to Unfurl a World Cup Flag
summary: Teams of stagehands work to coordinate how the giant flags are brought onto the field and displayed before each match. The prep takes days.

title: How Wealthy People Are Getting to World Cup Games
summary: The skies above the Hamptons are packed with choppers headed to games, as “inequality is taking it right in the face.”

title: D.O.J. Turns Over Evidence in Minnesota D.H.S. Shootings
summary: Investigators had for months refused to share information with their state counterparts about three shootings involving federal agents.

title: Authorities Investigate Fatal Shooting in Biddeford, Maine, Involving Federal Agents
summary: The killing occurred in Biddeford, about 20 miles south of Portland. The Trump administration has been conducting a surge of immigration enforcement.

title: Jay-Z Yankee Stadium Review: Three Nights of Legacy and Guest Stars
summary: The rap mogul convened his starriest collaborators, showed off his still-dazzling skills and made a convincing argument for his singular legacy.

title: Jay-Z Concert at Yankee Stadium Is Delayed for Hours
summary: The rapper apologized to fans when the show began after midnight on Monday, citing crowd safety issues.

title: In Turning to Trade School, Gen Z Confronts an Enduring Stigma
summary: Gen Z is increasingly turning to trade schools in hopes of future-proofing their careers against A.I. But getting their parents and peers on board can be a challenge.

title: “The Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire” Isn’t Saying What You Think It Is
summary: In the same year as America’s founding, Edward Gibbon wrote “Decline and Fall.” 250 years later, it still holds lessons for us.

title: Bison Attacks Tourist at Yellowstone National Park, Tossing Him Into the Air
summary: A man was hospitalized after a visibly agitated bull bison flipped him about eight feet in the air near a campground road.
